channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Housing Package Passed by Congress Has Wide Appeal, but It’s No Quick Fix
item.description: The first housing package in decades will take time to put into effect, testing the patience of families rankled by high prices.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm
item.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
item.media:description: The 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, which cleared Congress on Tuesday, aims to increase new construction.

item.title: Trump Meeting With G.O.P. Senators Puts Rifts on Display as He Upends Housing Bill
item.description: Hours before visiting the Capitol, the president scrapped plans to sign a bipartisan housing bill, railing against “bad Republicans” for resisting his demands to ram through new voting restrictions.
item.dc:creator: Carl Hulse and Michael Gold
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump has been in a simmering feud with Senator John Thune, Republican of South Dakota and the majority leader, over a range of political and policy issues.

item.title: Mamdani-Backed Candidates Sweep in NYC Democratic Primaries
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani shook the Democratic establishment by helping drive three progressive candidates to victory.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos
item.media:credit: Todd Heisler/The New York Times
item.media:description: Brad Lander, center right, was one of the three congressional candidates backed by Mayor Zohran Mandani who won their primaries on Tuesday.

item.title: Insurgency Over Incumbency: 7 Takeaways From New York’s Primaries
item.description: All three congressional candidates who were backed by Mayor Zohran Mamdani won in Tuesday’s primary, bolstering his political movement.
item.dc:creator: Maya King, Sally Goldenberg and Emma Goldberg
item.media:credit: Bing Guan for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani celebrated the victory of state Assemblywoman Claire Valdez, right, in a Democratic primary for a congressional seat.

item.title: Calling Democrats ‘Communists,’ Trump Keeps Close Eye on N.Y. Primaries
item.description: The president posted repeatedly into Wednesday morning to label Democrats as extreme, delight in his rivals’ losses and express some apparent envy of Mayor Zohran Mamdani.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump has developed a relatively friendly rapport with Mayor Zohran Mamdani, despite their vast differences.

item.title: Schlossberg’s Defeat Dampens Dream of a Renewed Camelot
item.description: Jack Schlossberg fell well short of his bid to win a highly contested Democratic primary in Manhattan, his campaign hurt by staff defections and missed meetings.
item.dc:creator: Jesse McKinley and Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
item.media:description: Jack Schlossberg, with his father, Edwin Schlossberg, right, saw his early polling lead evaporate.

item.title: Iran’s Loyalists Promote a Wider Nationalism, Unveiled Women Included
item.description: Government supporters are showing off new ties with alleged former dissidents in a bid to show that they can withstand enemies at home as well as abroad.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon and Sanam Mahoozi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A pro-government demonstration in Tehran last month where some of the women were partially veiled and some dressed more conservatively.

item.title: Europe Heat Wave Latest News: After France’s Hottest Day Ever, U.K. Approaches June Record
item.description: More than a dozen countries have issued urgent heat warnings. France saw its highest average temperature ever on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Nazaneen Ghaffar, Lynsey Chutel and Amelia Nierenberg
item.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Extreme Heat Across Europe Creates Divide Over Leaving Schools Open
item.description: Few schools in Britain or France have air-conditioning to keep children cool in soaring temperatures, leaving parents, teachers and officials divided about what to do.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg and Ségolène Le Stradic
item.media:credit: Jack Taylor/Reuters
item.media:description: Students eating ice pops during class at an elementary school in Grays, England, on Wednesday.

item.title: New Clues Point to Taylor Swift Wedding Event at M.S.G.
item.description: After weeks of speculation over Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding plans, a permit has been filed for an event at Madison Square Garden over the July 4 weekend.
item.dc:creator: Emma G. Fitzsimmons, Maria Cramer and Dana Rubinstein
item.media:credit: Gregory Shamus/Getty Images
item.media:description: There has been intense speculation about the details of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding ever since they announced their engagement in August 2025.

item.title: Pulte Trims Spy Agency, but Spares Bulk of Staff for Now
item.description: Six intelligence officials were fired and nearly four dozen others were sent back to their home agencies elsewhere, former officials said.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Dustin Volz
item.category: Office of the Director of National Intelligence
item.media:credit: Brendan Smialowski/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Current and former officials said Bill Pulte, the acting director of national intelligence, has considered much deeper cuts and bigger structural changes.

item.title: With a Proposed $250 Bill, Trump Reimagines America by Putting Himself on Its Money
item.description: Cash might be a relic, but — like royalty and emperors — President Trump still wants his face on American currency.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport

item.title: Dianna Russini Was an N.F.L. ‘Insider.’ Was She Also Out of Bounds?
item.description: Ms. Russini’s closeness to a coach, Mike Vrabel of the New England Patriots, led to her breakup with The Athletic and its parent, The New York Times Company.
item.dc:creator: Katherine Rosman and Ken Belson

item.title: Myanmar’s Civil War Has Become an Apocalypse
item.description: Overshadowed by conflicts in Ukraine, Gaza and Lebanon, the fighting in Myanmar’s isolated heartland has reached new depths.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Beech and Daniel Berehulak
item.category: Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:credit: Daniel Berehulak/The New York Times
item.media:description: On a forgotten frontline, the rebels fight deep in the jungle.

item.title: What to Know About Myanmar’s Chaotic War
item.description: The Southeast Asian nation recently experienced its highest monthly civilian death toll since the war began in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Beech
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat
item.media:credit: Daniel Berehulak/The New York Times
item.media:description: A rebel soldier at a frontline position in the Anyar region of Myanmar.

item.title: N.Y.P.D., F.B.I. Search Homes of Police Officials in Corruption Inquiries
item.description: The searches, by police internal affairs investigators and federal authorities, stemmed from earlier scrutiny of Jeffrey Maddrey, the former chief of the department, according to people with knowledge of the matter.
item.dc:creator: William K. Rashbaum, Maria Cramer, Chelsia Rose Marcius and Jonah E. Bromwich
item.media:credit: James Estrin/The New York Times
item.media:description: Federal agents and New York Police Department investigators have been scrutinizing police officials, including Jeffrey Maddrey, the former chief of department, in corruption inquiries.

item.title: Frank Carone, Longtime Eric Adams Associate, Is Arrested in Bribery Scheme
item.description: Brooklyn federal prosecutors are charging Mr. Carone, his brother and two others in a corruption indictment. He was the chief of staff to former Mayor Eric Adams.
item.dc:creator: William K. Rashbaum, Michael Rothfeld, Dana Rubinstein and Santul Nerkar
item.media:credit: Victor J. Blue for The New York Times
item.media:description: Frank Carone, former chief of staff to Mayor Eric Adams of New York, was taken into custody along with three others on federal corruption charges.

item.title: France Identifies Its First Case of Ebola
item.description: A doctor who had traveled to the Democratic Republic of Congo was infected, the French health ministry said. The authorities said the risk to the wider population was low.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel
item.media:credit: Arlette Bashizi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Health workers last month in the Democratic Republic of Congo, the center of an Ebola outbreak that has killed more than 200 people.

item.title: The Ebola Outbreak’s Central Mystery: Where Did This Virus Come From?
item.description: Scientists believe that the Bundibugyo virus persists in an animal species, occasionally spilling over into humans. But they have yet to identify the species.
item.dc:creator: Carl Zimmer
item.media:credit: Michel Lunanga/Getty Images

item.title: The Wearable Data Your Doctor Actually Wants
item.description: Wearables can offer rough information about a person’s health, experts say, but only some metrics are medically useful.
item.dc:creator: Simar Bajaj
item.media:credit: Hiroko Masuike/The New York Times

item.title: A Billionaire’s Basquiat Collection Goes Up at Pérez Art Museum Miami
item.description: Ken Griffin, the hedge fund manager, looks for artworks with a “wow” factor, never mind the number of zeros on the price tag. Ten of his Basquiats are on loan to the Pérez Art Museum Miami.
item.dc:creator: Brett Sokol and Ysa Pérez
item.category: Perez, Jorge M, Art Museum of Miami-Dade County
item.media:description: Art handlers with Basquiat’s “Pez Dispenser,” from 1984, at PAMM. The show represents the collector Ken Griffin’s coming out as a major player on the Miami art scene.

item.title: Thomas L. Friedman on the Clash at the Core of the Iran Deal
item.description: How the world views of Jared Kushner and the founder of the Islamic Republic of Iran can help explain the issue at the heart of the negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Thomas L. Friedman and Daniel J. Wakin
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Illustration by The New York Times; source photo by Kevin Dietsch/Getty Images

item.title: President Narcissus and the Fetid Reflecting Pool
item.description: Why the president’s latest renovation project is so irresistible and resonant.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Cottle
item.media:credit: Aaron Schwartz/Reuters

item.title: Your Questions, Answered
item.description: We’re taking on reader queries about the news.
item.dc:creator: Sam Sifton
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump and Elon Musk in the Oval Office last year.

item.title: Camp Mystic, Where 28 Died in Texas Floods, Files for Bankruptcy
item.description: Catastrophic floods that swept the Texas Hill Country last July killed 25 campers, two counselors and the camp’s executive director.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai
item.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
